question: A developer runs the command below to launch Camunda in Docker Compose on a custom port without the bundled Elasticsearch. What happens to `--port 9090` and `--disable-elasticsearch`?

options.text: Both flags are applied to the Docker Compose distribution started by `--docker`.
Both flags are silently ignored because `--docker` does not support additional C8Run options.
`c8run` aborts with an error because `--docker` is mutually exclusive with other configuration flags.
`--port 9090` is applied to the Docker stack, but `--disable-elasticsearch` is ignored.

a.text: The docs state that when `--docker` is used, additional C8Run options are not supported, so neither flag is applied to the Docker Compose stack.

b.text: The `--docker` flag downloads and runs the Camunda Docker Compose distribution, and per the docs, additional C8Run options are not supported and will be ignored.

c.text: `c8run` does not abort the command; the other flags are ignored rather than treated as a hard error.

d.text: There is no partial application — the docs say additional C8Run options are not supported when `--docker` is used, so `--port` is ignored along with the rest.

explanation: Per the c8run configuration options table, `--docker` downloads and runs the Camunda Docker Compose distribution as an easy shortcut. The docs explicitly note that additional C8Run options are not supported in this mode and will be ignored, so both `--port 9090` and `--disable-elasticsearch` have no effect on the resulting Docker Compose deployment.
